lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
The Arians are allowed by the Emperor to imprison Peter and to set Lucius over the See of Alexandria.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: The Arians are allowed by the Emperor to imprison Peter and to set Lucius over the See of Alexandria.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Upon this the Arians, emboldened by their knowledge of the emperor’s religious sentiments, again took courage, and without delay informed him of the circumstance.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He was then residing at Antioch.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Then indeed Euzoïus who presided over the Arians of that city, eagerly embracing the favorable opportunity thus presented, begged permission to go to Alexandria, for the purpose of putting Lucius the Arian in possession of the churches there.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The emperor acceded to this request, and as speedily as possible Euzoïus proceeded forthwith to Alexandria, attended by the imperial troops.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Magnus, also, the emperor’s treasurer, went with him.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Moreover an imperial mandate had been issued to Palladius, the governor of Egypt, enjoining him to aid them with a military force.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Wherefore having apprehended Peter, they cast him into prison; and after dispersing the rest of the clergy, they placed Lucius in the episcopal chair.
